Summary Goals Growth Glucose kinetics Exchange fluxes External concentrations Carbon budget What's growing? Mass balance How biomass is made Dark matter Doubling time Not covered yet

Nutrient-Growth Report

DM OFF  Baseline. MM glucose + environment depletion; LP homeostatic slacks unconstrained.
DM ON  Enforced. Baseline + dark-matter scale ∈ [0, 1] so net cell mass gain ≤ boundary imports.

Goals

Match the standard E. coli K-12 MG1655 doubling time of ~44 min in glucose minimal media (aerobic, 37 °C) while enforcing atomic mass conservation on the FBA output. Three design moves on this branch:

  1. Continuous glucose sensitivity — Michaelis-Menten uptake on boundary.external.GLC replaces the media-id lookup.
  2. Closed depletion loop — environment_update writes exchange deltas back to boundary.external using a configurable per-cell volume.
  3. Mass-conservation enforcement — a dark-matter pool scales LP output so net cell mass never exceeds boundary imports; growth freezes when carbon runs out.

Growth trajectory

Dry mass (top row) and instantaneous growth rate (bottom row) for both runs. The DM-OFF cell keeps gaining mass well past glucose depletion (red dotted line) because the LP's homeostatic slacks can synthesize biomass without a matching carbon import. The DM-ON cell flattens out once scale → 0 — growth literally cannot proceed without boundary carbon.

What picks up the slack when glucose cuts off?

Mean flux in snapshots with glucose (>1 mM) vs. post-depletion (<0.01 mM). DM OFF ramps other imports to feed the LP; DM ON scales them down once mass balance bites.

External nutrient concentrations

environment_update drains every boundary species over time. Ammonium typically depletes alongside glucose in the default 10 fL environment; phosphate, sulfate, and trace ions decay more slowly.

DM OFFbaseline (DM off)
DM ONenforced (DM on)

Carbon accounting

Per-step C flux (mmol C). Imports weighted by per-molecule C counts; biomass C from dry-mass delta at 48% C/g DCW. A red-filled region (C_out > C_in + biomass_C) flags pool-drain.

DM OFFbaseline (DM off)
DM ONenforced (DM on)

What's actually growing?

Dry-mass composition. In DM OFF every pool keeps accumulating past glucose depletion; in DM ON they flatten.

DM OFFbaseline (DM off)
DM ONenforced (DM on)

Mass-balance check

Closed cell invariant: Σ imports − Σ secretions = Σ dry-mass-gained (fg). Persistent negative deficit = LP synthesizing biomass faster than it imports mass.

DM OFFbaseline (DM off)
DM ONenforced (DM on)

How is biomass being manufactured?

modular_fba's homeostatic objective uses quadratic slack pseudofluxes (quadFractionFromUnity) bounded [−∞, +∞], so missed targets get filled in regardless of whether carbon is available. The built-in _massExchangeID pseudometabolite only audits exchange mass; bounding it to ~0 has no effect on dry-mass growth — confirming mass enters via the slacks in DM OFF. The DM-ON post-scale exists to correct this.

DM OFFbaseline (DM off)
DM ONenforced (DM on)

Dark-matter pool (diagnostic)

Per step, dark_matter += Σ Δbulk_mass − Σ Δexchange_mass using sim_data MWs (12,809 entries). Mass can't be created, so sustained upward drift = LP manufacturing mass.

DM OFFbaseline (DM off)
DM ONenforced (DM on)

Phase 2: enforcement at the metabolism step

DM ON wraps the FBA solve:

Δ cell_mass = Σ Δcount × MW   (from LP)
Δ boundary_mass = imports − secretions  (from exchange dict)
excess = Δ cell_mass − Δ boundary_mass
if excess > pool_fg: scale = (boundary + pool) / cell_mass

All LP deltas are multiplied by scale before bulk write — preserves stoichiometry, enforces Δ cell_mass ≤ boundary + pool. scale → 0 when carbon is gone and growth freezes.

DM OFFbaseline (DM off)
DM ONenforced (DM on)
Why does DM OFF keep growing after [GLC]ₑₓₜ hits zero?
  1. ~17 molecules have unconstrained exchange bounds (AMMONIUM, O₂, Pi, SULFATE, WATER, K⁺, MG²⁺, NA⁺, Cl⁻, Fe, Mn, Zn, Ca, Co, Ni, selenocysteine, CO₂); the LP pulls harder on these when glucose hits 0.
  2. The homeostatic objective pins internal metabolite concentrations to targets that rescale with growth — LP keeps producing internals regardless of upstream carbon.
  3. No carbon-balance constraint on biomass: with GLC off, the LP finds carbon via CO₂ fixation / reverse-TCA at reduced but nonzero flux.
DM ON eliminates all three at once via the post-scale. A proper mechanistic fix would bound the slack pseudofluxes or carbon-balance the balance.

Doubling time vs. K-12 target

Observed doubling time is fit from an exponential on dry-mass over the carbon-replete window ([GLC]ₑₓₜ > 1 mM). Target is the standard E. coli K-12 MG1655 value of 44 min in glucose minimal media (M9 + glucose, aerobic, 37 °C).

RunObserved (min)Target (min)Δ (min)
baseline (DM off)41.244-2.8
enforced (DM on)41.244-2.8

Doubling time vs initial [GLC] — does the cell stop without carbon?

Sweep across starting glucose concentrations, generated by python sweep_glucose.py (10 min × 5 levels × 2 modes, ~10 min wall). Two doubling-time fits per condition: early (0–300 s window, captures the initial-glucose regime) and full (whole sim, useful when growth is slow/null). Mass ratio is dry-mass at end ÷ dry-mass at start.

[GLC]₀ (mM)modedt early (min) dt full (min)mass ratio

22.0DM ON38.7115.01.114
5.0DM OFF38.442.31.275
5.0DM ON59.4166.71.085
1.0DM OFF38.442.31.275
1.0DM ON70.9182.51.078
0.1DM OFF38.442.31.275
0.1DM ON352.1257.71.038
0.0DM OFF38.641.01.284
0.0DM ON361.0254.01.038

Two clean signals:
1. DM OFF is glucose-blind. Doubling time is ~42 min regardless of starting glucose, including at 0 mM. Mass ratio is identical (1.275–1.284). The slack pseudofluxes manufacture growth with or without carbon.
2. DM ON gates growth on carbon. Doubling time stretches 115 → 167 → 182 → 254 min as initial glucose drops; mass ratio shrinks 1.114 → 1.038. At 0 mM, DM ON is effectively non-growing (1.038 over 15 min is residual small-molecule pool drain before scale fully clamps).

What this report does not cover yet

  • Non-glucose carbon sources: glycerol, gluconate, lactate. Requires media recipes + per-carbon v_max/K_m.
  • ppGpp-driven transition: mechanistic stationary- phase entry via the existing ppgpp_initiation pathway.
Generated by nutrient_growth_report.py · branch nutrient-growth. Calibration target: K-12 MG1655 glucose minimal media, ~44 min doubling time.
